title: Alorna Fort
tagline: Alorna Fort — heritage city of Goa

overview.short: Fort Santa Cruz de Alorna also known as Halarn Fort or Alorna Fort, is one of the oldest forts in Goa.
overview.description: Fort Santa Cruz de Alorna also known as Halarn Fort or Alorna Fort, is one of the oldest forts in Goa. It is situated 30 kilometers from the town of Mapusa. It was constructed by the Bhonsles of Sawantwadi in the 17th century, to defend against the Maratha attacks. As of 2010, the fort was in poor condition, and plans to restore it were under consideration.

topPlaces.name: Siege of Alorna
topPlaces.description: The siege of Alorna in 1746 was a military engagement that took place in India, between Portuguese forces under the command of the Viceroy of India, Dom Pedro Miguel de Almeida Portugal, and those of the Sawantwadi State then part of the Maratha Confederacy, which were defeated. It was the crowning achievement in the career of Dom Pedro Miguel as Viceroy of India, and as a reward for his services to the Portuguese Crown he was later attributed the title Marquis of Alorna.

topPlaces.name: Alorna
topPlaces.description: Alorna (Halarn) is a village in the Pernem Sub-Division of North Goa, India. Alorna is famous for the Alorna Fort.

topPlaces.name: Nadora
topPlaces.description: Nadora is a village at one end of the North Goa taluka, or sub-district, of Bardez. It is divided by the Colvale river from the northernmost Goa taluka of Pernem or Pednem.

topPlaces.name: Pirna, Goa
topPlaces.description: Pirna is a village in the North Goa sub-district or taluka of Bardez and is located in the northern edge of the sub-district. It is close to the Colvale river and is the home of the 150-metre long Pirna-Ozarim bridge at Thorli Chandai.

topPlaces.name: Goa Street Circuit
topPlaces.description: Goa Street Circuit is a street formula racing circuit in Goa. It hosted the Indian Racing League race in February 2026.

topPlaces.name: Latambarcem
topPlaces.description: Latambarcem is a village located in the North Goa district in the state of Goa, India. In 2001, the population was 6296.

topPlaces.name: Raneache Zuem
topPlaces.description: Raneache Zuem (transl. Rane's Island) is a small island situated within the North Goa district of Goa, a coastal state in India. It is near the village of Revora, with the two connected by a tourist spot called Zuem Bridge (Island Bridge).

topPlaces.name: Netarde
topPlaces.description: Netarde is a village in Sawantwadi Taluk, Sindhudurg District, Maharashtra, India. It is located along the state border with Goa, about 45 kilometres southeast of the district capital Oros, and 18 kilometres southeast of the taluk capital Sawantwadi.

It was the crowning achievement in the career of Dom Pedro Miguel as Viceroy of India, and as a reward for his services to the Portuguese Crown he was later attributed the title Marquis of Alorna.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Alorna Fort?
faq.a: The best time to visit Alorna Fort is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Alorna Fort?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Manohar International Airport, Mopa (North Goa) (6 km) and the nearest railway station is Vasco da Gama (Goa) (35 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Alorna Fort?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Alorna Fort at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Alorna Fort?
faq.a: Stays in Alorna Fort start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Alorna Fort?
faq.a: The top attractions are Siege of Alorna, Alorna, Nadora, Pirna, Goa, Goa Street Circuit.
